- 2359 AC1 → surfaces `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` in the batch outcome + `RECON:` line (covers AC4 clause 1).
- 2359 AC2 → test that `computed > 0 && applied == 0` fails, reproducing the link drop (covers AC4 clause 2 + SC3).

All other parent criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC1/AC2/SC1 → 8937 (schema SSOT + round-trip contract) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty); AC3/SC2 → 33d0 (collapse appliers); AC5/SC4 → 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards). 4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC4 + SC3 FULLY covered by this child; SC5/AC7 contributed; AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/SC1/SC2/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault; reportable per G3 — already raised on siblings 33d0/59ef/822a/8937, restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster including this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES the documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6

  - evidence: child 2359 AC4 "The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to THIS story's own canary test; 33d0 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 2359 itself for its owned criteria (AC4/SC3 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked on sibling tickets.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. Two high-confidence cross-child interaction findings; the remaining modes read benign.

---
FINDING 1 — conflicting assumption + ordering violation (2359 ↔ 33d0), shared mutable file state
- finding: 2359 and 33d0 (Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path) both rewrite the SAME outbound-apply region in the SAME files, and 2359's central deliverable assumes the production path is the BATCH applier — an assumption 33d0 is explicitly chartered to potentially invert, with no sequencing between them. 2359 plumbs `links_applied`/`comments_applied`/`labels_applied` counters + the `computed>0 && applied==0` canary into `batch_dispatch.py` `update_one` and the `applier.py` `RECON: batch_outcome` line. Repo confirms the batch path TODAY dispatches link/comment adds with NO per-sub-op counter (`batch_dispatch.py` ~L515-560 calls `set_relationship`/`add_comment` and silently continues; `applier.py` ~L816-820 `RECON: batch_outcome` emits only action/key/error). Meanwhile the counters + an existing no-op skip-guard ALREADY live in the typed leaf `apply_outbound.py::_apply_outbound_update` (~L324-392: `links_applied`, `labels_applied`, `comments_applied`, and the `not allowed and not labels_applied and not comments_applied and not links_applied` warning). 33d0's charter (33d0 What) says it may EITHER "delete the dead typed leaf _apply_outbound_update" OR "make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it." If 33d0 chooses the second option, the batch-path counter/canary plumbing 2359 just added is on a path that 33d0 reroutes or removes; if 33d0 lands first choosing that option, 2359 instruments a now-non-production path. Neither ticket declares a depends_on; both merely list the other as "Out of scope," which partitions WORK but not the SHARED FILE STATE or the WHICH-PATH-SURVIVES assumption.
  - criteria: 2359 AC1 (batch outcome + RECON line surface links/comments/labels_applied), 2359 AC2 (canary on the production path); parent AC3 (33d0) vs parent AC4/SC3 (2359)
  - location: 2359 What/Scope vs 33d0 What/Scope; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py (update_one, ~L515-560), applier.py (RECON batch_outcome, ~L816-820), apply_outbound.py (_apply_outbound_update counters + no-op guard, ~L324-392)
  - evidence: 2359 Scope.In = "outcome/telemetry plumbing in batch_dispatch.py / applier.py / reconcile.py"; 33d0 file_impact = batch_dispatch.py + applier.py + apply_outbound.py with reason "retire/consolidate typed leaf" / "routing"; 33d0 What = "Either delete the dead typed leaf … or make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it"; repo: batch path has no links_applied counter, typed leaf does (and has the no-op guard 2359 is re-deriving)
  - scenarios: (a) 33d0 picks "route batch through the typed leaf" and lands after 2359 → 2359's batch_dispatch counter/canary code is bypassed or deleted; the silent-no-op canary that "would have caught the link drop" no longer guards the live path. (b) 2359 and 33d0 land in either order with merge conflicts in the same ~40-line link/comment dispatch block of update_one. (c) 33d0 deletes apply_outbound.py's existing `links_applied`/no-op guard while 2359 still cites it as the "parity"/reference model.
  - impact: parent AC4/SC3 ("a silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary instead of passing green") could regress to green-on-the-live-path after 33d0 consolidates, exactly reinstating the bug-3f04 class on whichever path 33d0 does not carry the counter into; plus avoidable same-region merge conflict.
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions / ordering violation / implicit shared state
  - suggested_fix: sequence 33d0 BEFORE 2359 (collapse to one path first, then instrument the single survivor) and add an explicit 2359 depends_on 33d0; OR state in both tickets the canonical surviving production path so 2359 targets it unconditionally; OR fold the counter/canary requirement into 33d0's "single path" so the surviving applier carries telemetry by construction.

---
FINDING 2 — conflicting assumption: count-vs-list representation mismatch in the cited "parity" model
- finding: 2359 frames its deliverable as count-based sub-op telemetry plus a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` invariant, and justifies it as "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied." But the existing per-sub-op signals are NOT uniformly integer counts: the typed-leaf `labels_applied` is a LIST of label strings (e.g. `["+tag-alpha", "+tag-beta"]`, `["-stale-tag"]` — apply_outbound.py ~L389; tests/unit/.../test_applier_outbound_update.py L204/L233), while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints. 2359 AC1 asks for `labels_applied` in the same count-and-invariant frame as links/comments, but the canary's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` predicate and "the count invariant" (2359 AC4) are ill-defined over a list-valued `labels_applied` without an explicit normalization decision — and silently changing `labels_applied` from list to int would itself be a contract change to an existing payload key the inbound/typed-leaf tests assert on.
  - criteria: 2359 AC1 (surface labels_applied), 2359 AC2/AC4 (count invariant canary)
  - location: 2359 What/AC1; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_outbound.py ~L245-260, L389; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_update.py L88/L204/L233
  - evidence: apply_outbound.py result_payload "labels_applied": labels_applied where labels_applied is list[str]; tests assert `result.payload["labels_applied"] == ["+tag-alpha", "+tag-beta"]`; 2359 cites parity with inbound `links_applied` (an int)
  - scenarios: the canary computes `applied == 0` against a list for labels and either crashes/typo-passes, or 2359 silently flips labels_applied to an int and breaks existing typed-leaf/inbound label tests that 33d0 is separately retargeting.
  - impact: 2359 AC2's "count invariant" may be unimplementable as stated for labels without a representation decision; risk of breaking an existing asserted payload key, undercutting parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests / suite green."
  - checklist_item: cross-child modes — conflicting assumptions
  - suggested_fix: in 2359, specify the normalization (e.g. canary uses `len(labels_applied)` / a derived `labels_applied_count`, leaving the existing list key intact) and coordinate with 33d0 on the surviving applier's payload contract so the label signal is consistent across the one production path.

---
Modes that read BENIGN (not filed): scope overlap — 2359 and 33d0/822a each declare the other OUT and the work partitions cleanly aside from Finding 1's shared-file caveat; consumer impact — the added `RECON:` sub-op fields are additive and 2359 AC4 asserts the count invariant (not log strings), and 822a's probe-script audit keys on NOT-SYNCED guards, not on batch_outcome field set; dependency gap (inbound/8937/fe57) — none, 2359 needs no schema/fixture input; residual references — none. The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail governing NEW tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not re-filed here.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency re-audit (parent f89d × THIS child 2359), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and re-verified against the repo. CORROBORATES the two prior G4 findings on this ticket; both remain high-confidence and repo-grounded.

Repo re-verification:
- applier.py L816-820: the batch-path `RECON: batch_outcome` line emits only `action=…/key=…/error=…` (no per-sub-op counts); code comment calls it "the dominant outbound CREATE + UPDATE channel today" — this is the production path 2359 must instrument.
- apply_outbound.py (typed leaf): `links_applied`/`comments_applied` are ints (L325,278) but `labels_applied` is `list[str]` (L245,389); the existing no-op skip-guard (L372-378) already lives here, on the path 33d0 may delete OR make production ("Either delete the dead typed leaf … or make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it").
- apply_inbound.py L469/497: inbound `links_applied` is an int — the "parity" model 2359 cites is int-valued, reinforcing the labels list-vs-count mismatch (Finding 2).

Neither 2359 nor 33d0 declares a depends_on; they partition WORK (each lists the other as Out) but not the SHARED FILE STATE (batch_dispatch.py / applier.py) or the WHICH-PATH-SURVIVES assumption. Findings re-emitted unchanged: (1) conflicting-assumption + ordering + implicit-shared-state between 2359 and 33d0; (2) conflicting-assumption labels count-vs-list. Suggested resolution unchanged: sequence 33d0 before 2359 (add explicit depends_on) and/or fix the canonical surviving production path in both tickets and fold the counter/canary into 33d0's single survivor; specify label normalization (len()/derived count) so the invariant is well-defined without breaking the existing list-valued payload key.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), 4-bucket per owned parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and (correction below) NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" → 2359 AC1 (surfaces all three in the structured outbound batch outcome + RECON: line, parity with inbound applier). FULLY.
- Parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback" → 2359 AC2 (test asserts computed>0 && applied==0 fails, reproducing the link drop) + 2359 AC3 (fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn = flag flip, no other code change — exact match to parent Rollout & rollback). FULLY.
- Parent SC "A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 AC2. FULLY.
- Contributes to parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 2359 AC5 (canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings), and to parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 2359 AC4 (sync semantics unchanged, suite green).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does not own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT items (probe wiring → 822a; applier consolidation → 33d0) are consistent with the roster: AC SSOT-schema/fetcher-validation/differ-validation + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift" → 8937 (+ fe57 fixtures/fake, 59ef verified-fake honesty); AC "collapse to single production path" + AC "retired path deleted" + SC "exercised through production path" → 33d0; AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a; AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 (+ fe57).

CORRECTION TO PRIOR AUDITS ON THIS TICKET — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly names its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description states "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this criterion is FULLY covered, not partial. The prior "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates/ignored 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "sub-op telemetry surfaced", parent "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loud (warn-first)", parent SC "silent no-op fails canary" (all owned by this child); CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector tests" + parent AC "suite green"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/applier-collapse/round-trip/probe criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/33d0/822a); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. ONE high-confidence finding remains; the prior "ordering/which-path-survives" finding is now RESOLVED by the depends_on edge.

RESOLVED (no longer filed) — prior 2359↔33d0 ordering / implicit-shared-state / conflicting "which path survives" finding: 2359 now declares `depends_on 33d0` (present in deps; restated in the description "Sequencing: depends on story D (the single consolidated applier) so the counts come from one path"). The ordering edge that the earlier G4 audits said was missing now exists — 33d0 collapses to ONE production path first, then 2359 instruments the single survivor. Same-region co-edit of batch_dispatch.py/applier.py is now sequenced rather than concurrent. This mode reads benign under the current ticket state.

---
FINDING — conflicting assumptions: `labels_applied` is list-valued in the existing payload contract, but 2359's count-based canary invariant assumes an int (and 33d0 retargets that same payload contract)
- finding: 2359 AC1 surfaces `labels_applied` in the SAME count-and-invariant frame as `links_applied`/`comments_applied`, and 2359 AC2/AC5 define a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` "count invariant" canary, citing "parity with the inbound applier's existing `links_applied`." But the existing per-sub-op signals are NOT uniformly integer counts: `labels_applied` is a `list[str]` of decorated label strings, while `links_applied`/`comments_applied` (and inbound `links_applied`) are ints. The `applied == 0` predicate and "the count invariant" are ill-defined over a list-valued `labels_applied` without an explicit normalization decision — and silently flipping `labels_applied` from list to int would itself be a contract change to an existing payload key that current tests assert on by exact list contents. comments.body:
PASS 1 review [G6 — Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection]: 1 finding (MECHANISM edge-case under-specified in the PLAN PROSE), with strong affirmations. Grounded against the live code (batch_dispatch.py update_one, apply_inbound.py, apply_outbound.py) and the warn-first rollout.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE (G6.2): the plan solves the named problem, not a proxy. The defect was a silent sub-op no-op invisible in telemetry; the plan surfaces per-sub-op counts on the structured outcome (not only stderr) and asserts a canary that "would have caught the link drop." Repo confirms the gap is real: batch_dispatch.py update_one applies labels (L480-499), comments (L501-520), links (L522-559) best-effort with NO tally and returns only the scalar update_issue result (L561); applier.py's RECON: batch_outcome line (L818-820) emits only action/key/error. PASS.
- APPROACH SELECTION (G6.3): I generated two structurally-different alternatives and judge the chosen approach defensibly at-least-as-good, so I discard them: (a) a post-apply read-back/probe that re-fetches Jira to confirm each sub-op landed — strictly higher blast-radius (extra API calls per mutation), slower, and redundant with story F's probe wiring which 2359 correctly declares OUT; (b) raising on the first non-fatal sub-op failure inline instead of tallying — this loses the "applied M of N" partial-success signal and converts today's deliberately-non-fatal best-effort ops into pass-fatal ones, contradicting the existing soft-fail design (assignee/404/comment handlers in applier.py L707-792). The chosen count-and-canary-on-the-outcome approach is the best fit: additive, in-band, codebase-aligned with the inbound applier's existing links_applied (apply_inbound.py L469/L484/L497). PASS.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE (G6.4): present and explicit — "parity with the inbound applier's existing links_applied," the warn-first rollout rationale ("avoids hard-failing production syncs on a false positive"), the depends-on-D rationale ("so the counts come from one path"), and the YAGNI justification for not making the threshold configurable. PASS.
- MECHANISM — concurrency/ordering: no TOCTOU/atomicity hazard introduced; this is pure read-side telemetry plumbing + a guard over already-computed counts, sequenced after story D's single-path collapse via the declared depends_on 33d0. PASS.

FINDING (MECHANISM CORRECTNESS — G6.1, edge/empty case under-specified in the PLAN ITSELF):
- finding: The plan's canary predicate `computed > 0 && applied == 0` is under-specified for the link sub-op's legitimate already-present (dedup-skip) steady state, which would false-positive the canary as written in the prose. The live outbound link path skips an add that is ALREADY present in EITHER direction via `continue` BEFORE the apply call (batch_dispatch.py L550-551: `if existing_links is not None and (link_type, to_key) in existing_links: continue`). So a steady-state mutation that carries N>0 link adds all already present yields applied==0 with NO failure — exactly `computed>0 && applied==0` — yet nothing was dropped. Note this is also an ASYMMETRY with the cited "parity" model: inbound counts a re-applied link as applied (apply_inbound.py L483-484 calls rebar.link, whose redundant-link guard is INTERNAL so the call succeeds and links_applied += 1), whereas the outbound path skips before counting. The ticket's file_impact line resolves this ("update_one tracks per-sub-op computed(post-dedup)/applied counts") — i.e. computed is meant to be measured AFTER dedup-skip — but that load-bearing decision lives only in file_impact, NOT in the plan's What/AC prose under review. As written, an implementer reading only the plan body could compute `computed` from the raw mutation's sub-op count (pre-dedup) and ship a canary that false-positives every steady-state pass with already-present links — precisely the warn-first rollout's "false positive" the plan says it wants to avoid, but baked into the invariant's definition rather than caught by it.
  - criteria: G6 (mechanism correctness — edge/empty-case handling; the canary predicate must be well-defined over the already-applied/no-op-legitimate steady state)
  - location: plan What bullet 2 ("`computed > 0 && applied == 0`") and AC line 2; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py L530-559 (link dedup `continue` at L550-551), L561 (update_one returns only scalar result); apply_inbound.py L468-497 (inbound links_applied counts re-applied links)
  - evidence: batch_dispatch.py L550-551 skips already-present link adds before the apply call, so applied==0 with computed_raw>0 is a LEGITIMATE no-op, not a drop; apply_inbound.py L483-484 increments links_applied on a re-applied (redundant-guarded) link, so the cited inbound "parity" defines applied differently for the steady state; the corrective `computed(post-dedup)` semantics appear ONLY in the ticket file_impact, not in the plan body/AC under review
  - checklist_item: - [ ] State in the plan body/AC that `computed` for the canary is measured POST-dedup (i.e. computed = sub-ops actually attempted after already-present link adds are skipped), so a legitimate already-present steady state does not satisfy `computed > 0 && applied == 0`; and note the deliberate count-semantics difference vs the inbound applier (which counts a re-applied link as applied) so "parity" is not mis-read as "identical counting rule."
  - suggested_fix: In What bullet 2 / AC line 2, define computed as "sub-ops attempted after dedup/skip" (matching the file_impact's `computed(post-dedup)`); add an AC case asserting the canary does NOT fire when all N link adds are already present (applied==0 because computed==0 post-dedup), alongside the existing AC case asserting it DOES fire on a true drop (computed>0, applied==0). This makes the warn-first baseline genuinely "zero warnings" instead of warning on every steady-state pass.

Not raised under G6 (out of scope or covered elsewhere): the labels_applied list-vs-int representation mismatch is already tracked as a G4 cross-child finding on this ticket; the warn-first→hard-fail flag flip and "sync semantics unchanged" are sound as stated.

PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection): COACHING, 2 findings + affirmations.

Mechanism grounded against live code:
- `apply_inbound.py:469-497` already returns `links_applied` (counts SUCCESSFUL applies, +1 per rebar.link, line 484) — the inbound-parity claim is accurate.
- `apply_outbound.py:125-161` delegates to `batch_dispatch.update_one`, which returns ONLY the scalar `result` dict-or-None (`batch_dispatch.py:561`); the label/comment/link sub-op loops (lines 480-559) increment NO counter today. So the counts the plan wants do not yet exist on the batch path — they must be instrumented, not merely surfaced.
- `applier.py:818-821` emits the `RECON: batch_outcome` line; that is the correct attach point for the new counts.

FINDING 1 (mechanism — false-positive in the canary invariant): outbound link dispatch intentionally SKIPS already-present links via the dedup probe (`batch_dispatch.py:550-551`: `if (link_type, to_key) in existing_links: continue`). On a steady-state re-sync where every link-add in the payload is already present, `computed > 0` (links in payload) but `applied == 0` (all skipped) — a LEGITIMATE idempotent no-op, not the silent-drop bug. The plan's invariant `computed > 0 && applied == 0` will flag this as a failure. The inbound side has the same shape (rebar.link's redundant-link guard, `apply_inbound.py:465-466`). The warn-first rollout (AC#3) reduces blast radius but does not make the invariant correct — a baseline pass over issues with pre-existing links will emit these warnings forever and block promotion to hard-fail. The plan should distinguish "computed but skipped-as-already-present" from "computed but dropped/failed" so the canary fires on the bug, not on idempotency. This is the load-bearing mechanism detail; please pin down what `applied` counts (successful dispatch attempts vs. net store mutations) and how dedup-skips are excluded from the no-op condition.
  - [ ] Define the canary so dedup-skipped sub-ops (already-present links: `batch_dispatch.py:550-551`; inbound redundant-link guard) do NOT trip `computed > 0 && applied == 0`; specify whether `applied` counts successful-dispatch-OR-already-present vs. net mutations, so an idempotent re-sync stays green while a true silent drop fails.

FINDING 2 (approach selection / dependency-sequencing): the plan asserts "the counts come from one path" and depends on story D (consolidated applier). But D's job is single-source-of-truth for the dispatch leaf, whereas THIS story must add NEW counters inside the sub-op loops (`batch_dispatch.py:480-559`) that don't exist regardless of D. The dependency on D is defensible (avoids instrumenting two divergent paths), but the plan should confirm the counter instrumentation lives in `update_one`/the consolidated applier and is THREADED OUT (today `update_one` returns only the scalar result; a counts dict/return-shape change is required). An alternative — accumulate counts in the existing `comment_errors`-style out-param the caller already passes (`applier.py:720-722`) — is structurally lighter (no return-shape change, mirrors the proven `comment_errors` plumbing) and may be at-least-as-good. Consider naming the carrier (return-shape change vs. out-param like `comment_errors`) so the implementer doesn't pick by accident.
  - [ ] State HOW sub-op counts are threaded from `update_one` (which returns only the scalar `result` today, `batch_dispatch.py:561`) to the batch outcome — return-shape change vs. an out-param mirroring the existing `comment_errors` accumulator (`applier.py:720-722`); justify the choice.

Affirmations (G6 PASSES):
- Fitness-for-purpose: the named problem (silent sub-op no-op leaves the pass green) is solved directly by surfacing per-sub-op counts + a count-based canary; not a proxy. The AC's anti-change-detector clause ("asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings") is exactly right and well-grounded.
- Positive rationale present: parity-with-inbound-`links_applied`, single-source-via-story-D, and warn-first-to-avoid-false-positive-hard-fails are all stated as affirmative why-this-fits reasons.
- Approach selection: warn-first-behind-a-flag with flag-flip promotion/reversion is a sound rollout for a fail-loud invariant; the YAGNI scope cut (no configurable threshold) is justified. No clearly-superior structural alternative was missed at the rollout level.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Both link bugs were invisible in telemetry: the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None` and "applied N of N" but never the per-sub-op counts, so a link/comment/label that silently no-ops (or fails non-fatally, logged only to stderr) leaves the pass green. The inbound applier already returns `links_applied`; the outbound batch path does not. Make a silent no-op fail loudly — safely.

## What

- Surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied` in the **structured outbound batch outcome** (queryable, not only logged) and the `RECON:` line — parity with the inbound applier's existing `links_applied`.
- Add a **silent-no-op canary**: when a mutation carries N sub-ops of a kind but `applied == 0`, that is a failure condition (`computed > 0 && applied == 0`), surfaced in the outcome/telemetry and asserted by a test that would have caught the link drop.
- **Rollout safety:** the fail-loud invariant lands **warn-first** — emit telemetry + a `WARNING` behind a flag/canary — and is promoted to hard-fail only after a baseline pass that emits zero such warnings. Reverting to warn is a flag flip, no other code change. This avoids hard-failing production syncs on a false positive.
- Sequencing: depends on story D (the single consolidated applier) so the counts come from one path.
- Out of scope (justified): making the threshold itself configurable — the simple `computed > 0 && applied == 0` invariant IS the contract; only enforce-vs-warn is flagged. A configurable threshold is YAGNI.

## Scope

- In: outcome/telemetry plumbing in `batch_dispatch.py` / `applier.py` / `reconcile.py`; the canary test; the warn-first flag.
- Out: the probe wiring (story F); applier consolidation (story D).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The structured outbound batch outcome and the `RECON:` line surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied`.
- [ ] A test asserts that a mutation with computed sub-ops > 0 but applied == 0 fails (reproduces what the link drop would have triggered).
- [ ] The fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first (telemetry + `WARNING`) behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn are a flag flip with no other code change.
- [ ] Sync semantics are otherwise unchanged (telemetry + guard only); the full reconciler suite is green.
- [ ] The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector).
